text: Literally, ‘a horn, the son of oil’. The word ‘horn’ is generally interpreted here as a hill; but a rocky crag such as the word would indicate would be no place for a vineyard.

This is the only possible way of translating the Hebrew text as it stands; but it seems likely that the word ‘nations’ has been written, through a copyist’s error, instead of ‘nation’. In that case the passage would run quite simply: ‘And now he will raise a signal to a nation that is far away; he will whistle it up …’ etc.
